reviews.review_description:
Accessed through my Horizon+ subscription, I have a lot of games to try and I'm happy I gave this a go, I just unfortunately won't have long enough to complete the campaign and because I have so many other games from purchasing and the subscription I'm not motivated to complete the game, knowing I will soon need to purchase it as it's being pulled from the catalog. 

In saying that, it's a darn fun game to play, I would say it's somewhat repetitive however that can be said for most games. This one does a decent job of giving you freedom to try things differently and play your way, as others have said the graphics aren't insanely good but the optimisation is so you have a great sense of presence.

Gameplay mechanics are good so it's very playable, I didn't experience discomfort (except when I was playing laying down, so expected) 

I don't know what the purchase price is (something Meta needs to fix for subscribers) so I can't recommend to purchase based on its price, also I won't have it long enough to finish the campaign so I can't say how long the game is either. However based on my experience I do recommend the game as enjoyable and a good addition to your library. 

From what time I put into it the game is linear, so if you prefer open world exploration there are better options, but it isn't on rails either so you do still have freedom to search for secrets and maybe Easter eggs. 

I would buy it on sale or if it came back into H+ I'd play again, sad to see it leaving the rotation so soon after I claimed it.

(Played on Quest 3) I recently finished the campaign mode and felt I had to write a review simply because of how much fun I had with this. It's seriously good on so many levels. No, it doesn't have killer graphics (elements rage from serviceable to pretty good), but what's there, combined with the high quality in sound design, music, interaction mechanics, variety of options, and sheer immersive joyful play pushes this to 5 stars. Movement is intuitive and smooth; switching tools and weapons and using each is smooth and logical and unlocks the chance to develop your own playstyles and solutions; and the verticality of later levels, the amount of jumping, climbing, fighting, mining, problem solving... honestly, it's a very complete package.
If the devs ever do a sequel someday (ideally with massively ramped-up visuals - shock us!), it'd be a day-one purchase for me.
Note: there are some issues - aliens clipping into walls, some scenery/minables vanishing, etc. but given how good the game consistently is, and how much sheer fun I was having they were a very minor momentary annoyance at most.
Special mention for the voice actor who plays Jonathan - I actually wondered if Matt Berry (Stephen Toast) was voicing it; it's a d@mn fine impersonation of him and adds just enough humor to the mix without being heavy-handed.
5/5 (with a quarter point off for graphics, but that's more a hardware limitation, assuming they needed this to be playable on Quest 2 also).

reviews.review_title: This game deleted all my missions 😒😠😢
reviews.review_description: The "help us improve/feedback" page popped up, i pressed accept/decline and it deleted ALL my missions had to start from mission 1 (tutorial). This happened 3 times. Whenever i pass the "follow the ghost/spirit" mission the game deletes all my missions. You cannot skip over this page because it covers the selection buttons entirely. Never had this problem with any other game i purchased. The Game is a blast of fun and has potential but to keep starting over back to mission 1/Level1 is frustrating. Would of been 5 STARS and really wanted to finish the last mission but i will have to uninstall and find another game to play.😒😒

developer_response.body: Hey Kurt, that pop up should only appear once, the fact that it keeps happening seems to indicate your device is not saving any data at all. It might be the memory is full, but to solve this issue we need a little more info. Could you please contact us at support@thebinarymill.com or if you prefer jump on our discord at discord.gg/thebinarymill.com

reviews.review_description:
I recently completed my first solo campaign playthrough on the normal difficulty. The graphics overall are solid with the lighting and colour throughout the caverns being a particular highlight. The voice acting and dialogue is good, but I expected this from the devs, having played their previous game, Resist (which is also worth checking out, BTW).

There's an extensive upgrade system that will take a while to fully exhaust for the completionists out there. It's good fun dual wielding weapons, pickaxes, and laser mining tools when taking on the insect bad guys, not forgetting the grenades as well. The mining for resources while exploring the levels is also oddly addictive to a degree.

Where the game falls down for me slightly is that it did start to feel a bit repetitive after a while. Maybe this is partly my fault as I did a lot of the side missions gathering resources so I could invest in weapon and tool upgrades. It got to the point where I felt a bit overpowered, and there was nothing left for me to upgrade. Yes, I was playing on normal difficulty level, which I could change, but 'normal' became a bit too 'easy'. I breezed through the final boss battle.

The one other thing I'd note is that I did encounter some odd glitches now and again. Enemies no-clipping and disappearing; I could hear them through walls or ground but couldn't get to them. In some instances, I could only throw a grenade in their general direction and hope the blast extended beyond my reach. Sometimes, large chunks of scenery would completely disappear. E.g. on one of the underwater caverns, I could cross a large space, but when I turned around, the scenery would reappear, which I would then have to swim around. Another odd glitch was when I jumped through a waterfall. When I got to the other side on what should have been dry land, the game still thought I was underwater so I could effectively levitate. There were a few oddities like this, but most were not game breaking.

Thanks to the devs for an overall solid experience. IF you're still making VR games with all the recent studio closures or redirection to flatscreen gaming only, then I'll be sure to check out your future efforts. Hopefully, you can continue in the VR space.

Great game! I've had this one wishlisted for a long time but just hadn't pulled the trigger on it. Finally an opportunity came up to play it and I was not disappointed. Visuals are great, I'm playing on Quest 3. Combat is fun, starts off slow but ramps up as you go along. Currently making my way through the campaign with my brother. We've done pretty much all the levels on Insane difficulty together since we like a challenge. Highly recommend this game to everyone, it's a good time.

This is coming from 2 players that beat the campaign from start to finish. 
-positives! 
- Platforming mechanics are cool! 
- Lots of tools and upgrades.
- Combat is decent. 
- The atmosphere is excellent
- Fun with friends! 

-Needs improvement. 
- The ship is never utilized past the beginning cutscenes. You should have missions where you can use the ship. 
- Mining ore is enjoyable, but for a game that's heavily reliant on mining, you should be able to mine the terrain more. Like certain areas you can freely dig around. 
- Swimming. In the trailer it showed the player using their hands to swim, but you can't actually swim forward if you do this, you have to use the controller thumbstick which was disappointing. 
- Boss fights were lackluster (especially the final) they felt like copy+paste and didn't feel especially dynamic. 
-the enemy creeps were cool design wise, but after a while it felt copy+paste too! Little variety. 
- The creeps AI was clumsy. They got stuck a lot.
- The storyline is confusing at times. 

We both really enjoyed the game, but overall this felt unfinished, but it was a worthwhile experience! 
If the previously mentioned aspects of the game that needs to be fixed are ever adjusted, it would be a 10/10 slam dunk! :)

reviews.review_title: Great game ruined by broken mechanics
reviews.review_description: The story is great, the exploration and game loop is fun, and the harvesting mechanics for some resources are completely broken. I've already had to restart to get some scripts to trigger properly and now this time it won't let me harvest some resource types at all. I'm done.

reviews.review_title: wanted to rate five stars but no can do
reviews.review_description: a pretty fun game with great graphics. there's several things that hold it back from being a great game though. there are so many bugs, from the enemy's clipping into the ground, to the giant stone guys just freezing into place, from you just hovering infinitely, this is probably one of the buggies games i've ever played. also the difficulty is pretty extreme on the normal difficulty but i enjoy the challenge. but it can be really frustrating when you die and there's a huge reset of like 10-15 minutes of progress. the checkpoint system is not well done. if you can get over those things then it actually is a really fun game and i'm enjoying it.

I absolutely love this game. it really reminds me of Subnautica, but set in space. I just wish more effort had gone into multiplayer matchmaking, since I don’t have many friends to play co-op games with.
Loved it! We need a sequel, follow up, or DLC content with more caves to explore! This is a fun game either solo or co-op and I wish we had more true co-op games like Into Black. Highly recommend!

It's really fun. No bug issues. Great controls. Great immersion. Epic visuals and use of lighting for standalone. Multiplayer works really well. Cool main story. Easily dozens of hours of playtime. Feels AAA. A lot of choice in experience as far as difficulty and time commitment (choose difficulty on each run, choose to do a 30 min+ excursion or just pop in for 5-10 minutes to farm). Ticks all the great game boxes.

If you played deep rock galactic and looking for a similar experience, this is it. It has darkness, mining and guns. Best played with friends. Definitely has a lot going on for it and will be playing this for a while. ROCK AND STONE BROTHERS!!

It's essentially a story based Deep Rock Galactic for VR and I love it for it.

Pro:
* The sense of scale is just stunning
* Artwork is minimalistic, but looks real good
* The colors, man
* All VR features feel great, the guns, the movement, the jumping, the map, the whip to interact with things, etc
* Voice acting is shockingly good, like AAA game level good

Con:
* The volume of different effects varies too much, even after fine tuning it with the volume sliders. Eg when you hit a rock, especially in the sweet spot, is insanely loud. This definitely needs to be addressed, I almost dropped the game because of it.
* Enemy movement is kinda goofy
* Game tends to lag out if too much is going on
* Took me half the game to realize you dont need to craft every intermediate material by hand
* Bossfights are a bit repetitive

9/10
highly recommended

This game provides an engaging experience during the first half of the campaign, immersing you in a variety of environments and tough enemies. Early upgrades feel rewarding and make a noticeable difference. However, as you move further along, the gameplay starts to feel repetitive—revisiting the same locations, facing similar enemies, and using the same weapons. Upgrades become less clear and offer only minor changes. Still, I’d recommend giving it a shot. Once the novelty wears off and boredom kicks in, it's okay to put it down: nothing really changes, not even the final boss. After defeating the final boss, the game comes to an end, and the main character decides to stay and explore (exploit) the planet further. I expected more from the end and the story.

I’m relatively new to “gaming”, and a bit over 3 years with VR. What a blast this game is! The visuals are beautiful and coupled with a compelling storyline. Had to learn a ton as I progressed as the tutorial isn’t super helpful for some things. The ending….a bit underwhelming when you are trying to build a failed engine but don’t get the opportunity to actually fly off into the sunset. The space levels still blow me away…truly felt untethered and floating in the air, left me breathless. Get this game, no regrets.

This game is an excellent value. The gameplay and narrative are terrific and the immersion is extremely fun. I feel like this is a good model of how more inexpensive games, so maybe games that are produced on a lower budget(?) Should be done. It's an excellent example of a developer taking basic resources and creating something  exceptional.
I found this game to have really good graphics for a vr game and fun to play.
I'm 10 hours into the campaign and having a blast. Haven't even touched either of the multiplayer modes yet. We'll worth the money

This is the best game Ive ever played on quest 3.

Great job

reviews.review_title: It was just perfect for me
reviews.review_description: To be honest, I cannot pinpoint what was with this game that made me spend as much time as possible with it until I played it through. The story, the visuals, the gameplay, the voice acting, the mechanics - for me it's actually just perfect.

reviews.review_title: Into Black is one of the best games ===
reviews.review_description: Into Black === I’ve been playing VR games for 5 years and this one is a lot of fun.   Everything works smoothly = graphics are well done ,  voice acting is excellent, and shooting at flying bugs and land critters , also a lot of fun.    I did turn off the  “ busy “ background music after 20 minutes of playing.   Exploring everything in the caves is relaxing and === yessss.

Invite doesn’t work at all
Progression lost constantly 
Game kicks constantly
Looks like I’ll be refunding this. 
Was looking forward to playing but after fighting with co op at least twice a mission and having to constantly restart -it’s really not worth it. 
Don’t know if this was abandoned or is still getting updates. 
It seemed like it would’ve been neat if co op just was actually tested and made stable before launching the game.

This was a fun game. The environment is absolutely beautiful. Even though you are walking through the inner core of a planet, it never gets boring or repetitive, and each mission is different. There are plenty of challenges along the way. The dialogue is entertaining as well. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Amazing single player or co-op experienc
reviews.review_description:
There are very few games for the Quest that are narrative driven, fun and have co-op options. I like playing with my retired father sometimes, and anything too complicated makes it unplayable for him. 

This game is just fun, simple controls, enough action to cause some tension, but also a large chunk of exploration and mining for resources to make the game last a bit longer. There are oodles of upgrades to unlock that actually modify your gameplay, so there is a reason to actually mine without making it feel like a chore. Besides Arizona Sunshine, this is probably my favorite game so far... And i played a LOT of games hahaha. 

The only downsides is the limited use of the axe, and there is a fair bit of buggy/glitchy terrain where there are either gaps you can look through the map, or even get stuck in/behind, which happened a handful of times, also bug sometimes get stuck behind walls or below floors which sometimes leads to you getting attacked without a way to defend or defeat them...
Really love the game perhaps a new story Plus mode or more story to the game everyone really play this game though it's great it is still getting updates

This is an incredible VR title that stands apart in the Quest catalogue. This title is as if Destiny and No Man’s Sky collaborated on a VR experience. 

The mechanics are smooth as butter and addicting. This is an excellent exploration, mining, crafting, fps battling experience.

Unreal experience, unreal price.


Update: the multiplayer needs much work hard to get I to lobbies and why can't you add anyone I met a friend but I can never talk to him again cause I can't meet him😔
This game is very good but if you bought it before in 2024 you know what we need..    OUR F###### MONEY BACK IT COST LIKE 24.99$ IN 2024
Good optimization, beautiful graphics it’s really enjoyable to play this game!

Good game
















































































It's good game

This game is exceptionally fun even after my second play through back to back. The movement options offered feel incredible in vr. Paired with the simple but colorful well crafted big scale environment, this makes exploring extremely addictive. Looting and shooting feels very satisfying, also thanks to the well done sound effects. If there is ever a part two planned, I would wish for more replayable content (maybe a rogue like in a procedual generated environment) because I want to keep zipping around and looting forever!

I love playing this one. You have everything: mining, crafting, killing, get resources, puzzles, discovering new locations, caves,….everything that a good game has to have. 
You can set the difficulty, what makes it also very playable. 
And you can play in coop, to defeat all those creatures.

Don’t know (yet) how long the games is, but I played already a lot of hours.

Definitely a must have, when you like those kind of games.

This game is leaving H+ soon. It's been on my radar for a minute. So I finally tried it out. I really wish I had jumped in sooner 😔 I'm only an hour or so in but am really enjoying it! Going to buy it if I don't finish in time
Loving it. Great gameplay. A little story in there, what i liked. Nice way to upgrade a lot. Great ambiance. Multiplayer needs some work, but gge co-op is fantastic

Into Black is one of the few games I've played from start to finish. It even stole my attention from Arken Age (which I LOVE dearly). After I played and finished Resist, I became obsessed with The Binary Mill and sought out any and all games created by them. I eagerly look forward to more amazing masterpieces from The Binary Mill. 💯

P.S. Please give us a part 2!!!!

A wonderfully colourful game with excellent environments and very good voice acting too. I've just started but already like it a lot.. You can lots of fun and depending on difficulty settings, you can have a more relaxed or more frenetic experience. Recommended!

The multiplayer function no longer works. It says Location is not joinable at this time when joining game invites. PLEASE FIX....I LOVE THIS GAME OTHERWISE!! Once fixed I will update my review to 5 stars. Thanks

I have uninstalled and reinstalled this game two times. And I always get stuck in the same spot in the tutorial. Once fixing the power matrix with the little puzzle, nothing happens. The robot doesn’t talk to me and I can still walk around the ship and I could walk through the closed doors. I have tried this several times with different profiles and always get stuck at the same spot. I have looked this up on Google and found that people have had the same problem, but have no cure for it. Any help would be grateful. ￼

developer_response.body: Hi there, sorry to hear you're having issues getting through the tutorial. We're still tracking down the exact cause of this issue, but recently we found asking a user to delete all cloud save data from their device then playing from a fresh profile to help. We think it's something to do with users who previously tried the demo and stopped at a certain point of progress. If you'd like more help or want to discuss things in more depth please send us an email at support@thebinarymill.com

reviews.review_description: Intense, story driven, action, and phenomenal voice acting - what more can you want?
Starting this game was stunning and it’s beautiful to look at throughout all of what I’ve seen so far. I have nice games but I don’t play them. I play this game.

reviews.review_title: Great game with a few issues
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed playing this game. I finished it fairly quickly because it was intriguing. Exploration was fun. This game is definitely worth it for the cheap price it has. I liked it for the most part but there are some things that need improving 
For example, the story was okay but the ending was very underwhelming unfortunately. The bosses were easy and very repetitive (the final boss was the easiest one imo). When you progress further into the game, the enemies start to become more of a nuisance because you always fight the same enemies. Also, the occasional bugs are pretty annoying (like, enemies glitching into the wall).
Still, even with these complaints, I did like playing this game. I am very excited to see what's next from this game dev company and if these issues will be fixed, then I'm sure the next game will be amazing
This was great. It seemed to have just the right mix of charm, mechanics, action, and story. Highly recommended.

This is a gorgeous game that hits all of the right notes as a VR title.  The gameplay looks awesome, sounds awesome, and is fun to play.  This is the first game I've had in a while where I look forward to playing every time I put on my Quest.
For a 4 and a half hour game it sure was long,  it took me about 2 weeks to finish it lol Anyway it was a good game.  Good graphics, I didn't experience too many glitches.

If one of you dies, good luck trying to revive them due to bugs. Unfortunately this ruins what would be a great coop game thaty wife and I were looking forward to playing together.

Bugs we immediately encountered:

- Wife died, I figured out the mini game, got the successful revive indicator but the body was still there. Weirdly, wife was able to throw objects and the yellow revive beams were visible from where her hands would have been if alive. Like she was trying to revive herself. But she was still dead. We had to restart. 

- I fell into lava, was not dead under the lava, jumped out but then landed on it and died. My body was then below lava and there was no way for my wife to reach me. Throwing health didn't work because it would break on the surface of the lava. Unstuck didn't work either. I just stayed there. Wife then died and the mission exited as abandoned. Super frustrating. 

So basically, based on our experience you can play the game together provided none of you die, which is unacceptable. We had a 100% bug rate so far on deaths and since the purpose of playing the game was for co-op, I can't recommend this game (hence the rating) and am disappointed in what seems to be a lack of basic testing.

There is also no tutorial or tip for co-op. For example, it took a while to figure out what to do to (try) to revive. Not having a tip screen like for all other first encounters with a game mechanic suggests coop isn't a dev priority, which is a real shame.

Info for devs: We played on normal difficulty, this was all on the very first mission.

developer_response.body:
Hey Flo, 

Apologies for what sounds like a really rough session in the game. I'm sure we can get to the bottom of what happened, and hopefully get everything working correctly for you. If you can reach out to us at support@thebinarymill.com or on our discord at discord.gg/thebinarymill that will be the best way to go through these issues, but for now I'll report our findings here.

Starting with the whip-reviving mini game, we tested this in house just now and it appears to be working on our end. Normally when doing the game you keep the marker in the safe zone until the green circle fills, which it sounds like you figured out. However, once that green circle fills, you must then yank back on the whip, like when pulling mushrooms. Is it possible you missed this step?

As for the lava, this might just be a missing section of geometry you found and fell though. If you could provide us a video or more detailed description of where it is we'll patch it up ASAP, but in the mean time there is an 'unstuck' button in the pause menu which should move you back to a safer piece of ground, should you hit an issue like this again.

I hope this goes some way towards helping you out, and that you're willing to give the game another chance. As I said before, please reach out to us and we'll be able to provide much more detailed feedback and assistance going forwards.

reviews.review_title: This is my favorite vr game!
reviews.review_description: I love this game and its graphics it’s challenging but not too challenging. Excellent game 5/5

Get the game. It is a fun adventure to experience, collecting resources, getting components, defending yourself from ugly bugs. And the more people that play the sooner the matchmaking so help me out and play the game. Its fun believe me.
thanks for making the game, Devs.
It's a wonderful game to play with a friend (or several of them). It takes a while to really get into it and understand it fully but it's overall very rewarding 😊

I'm on my third playthrough (one solo and two co-op), so clearly I'm enjoying this game. It's not perfect, there are a few bugs, but the devs have been whittling them down over the past year and none have been game breaking for me. The graphics are really nice and Johnathan is a hoot to listen to. I still haven't explored the other mode yet, so after finishing the current playthrough, I'll explore that.
J adoreeeee
I do love the resource gathering and facing off aliens ￼￼

Loving the game play amazing graphics . can wait to play more had 2hr fun so far .
Definitely recommend this game if you like adventure games. 👍  please give us more i miss playing and love playing with friends
Did you hear the tragedy of darth seal the wise? It’s not one the whales would tell you ￼
A game with a gameplay as those of a time, or at least it want to be like that. Good graphic and sound effects/music. Some bugs those of the game apart.
So good
Just sayin 😊😁
Continuously killed and having to start over time and time again with impossible situations.

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear you're having a rough time with the difficulty of the game. If you'd like to make things a little easier there is a difficulty option for each mission, you can see it on the right-side panel when at the mission select terminal in the center of the ship. Hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: I love this game easy to pick up and play also keeps u on ur toes
Good name
I was surprised how good it was and how I enjoyed it.

I think the graphics are up there with the best.
And I love the narration and game play.
This is a great game I think it is the best game I have played in a while I really think ther should be a dlc or a sequel

Stuck at the beginning of the game because the giant worm that’s supposed to chase you back to the ship doesn’t appear but the hole does
What do I do? 

￼

developer_response.body:
Hi, sorry to hear you're having trouble with the intro mission. This was a bug reported a few weeks ago, but we've since patched it. Can you make sure your game is updated to the latest version and try again? If you click the three dots in the upper left corner of the game icon in your library, scroll down to the information section and click the button to see more information, you'll be able to see the version code. That number should be 1.7.024.338

If you're still having trouble or would like more help, please reach out to us at support@thebinarymill.com

reviews.review_description: Bravo to the developers! Waiting for more great stuff in the future! Many Kudos!
A game that i keep going back too.

reviews.review_title: This is what VR is for 😎
reviews.review_description:
Bravo to the Devs. This is a well balanced game that is highly immersive. The atmospheres are magical and the movement is fantastic. The story is tastefully present enough to keep the adventure progressing without being burdensome. Traversing is a delight. The combat is fun. I love this game.

PERFECT:
Movement is superb. They avoided jarringly harsh start & stops when moving that so, so many apps fail to consider. Jumping and dashing are smooth and intuitive. Reminds me of the console game Ori.

Atmosphere. There’s a true sense of epic scale with a variety of dark and mysterious environments. The flare system is surprisingly fun and I find it makes me take moments to pause and appreciate the experience.

Load out. Customizing the controls is a core feature that makes this game so much fun.

NOT SO GREAT:
The pickaxe. It’s the only weapon that moves too quickly while handling like it’s made of photons and it ruins the immersion.

The upgrade system. The bridge between raw materials > components > upgrades is broken by not having descriptions in the upgrade crafting menu. I have run to the load out bay to read the description then return to the crafting bay. This really disrupts the flow of memorizing materials and components to plan for upgrades since I can’t remember what I’m saving for. This is by far the weakest point of the game for me. I want to know what the upgrade will do with stats that give me more than “increases damage by 1.2” … how much damage is that? How much damage do I currently do? It’s just a vague idea that doesn’t really mean anything so I can’t really weigh it against other options. Since the materials for upgrades are real commitments, knowing which one to focus on is not intuitive at all.

All that being said, my hat’s off to the Devs. I love this game. One of my favorites to-date.
The reason why i say why is because why arent any more games as good as this. Super fun and even multiplayer!

reviews.review_title: Wow. Most engrossing game I've played
reviews.review_description: I've had an Oculus 3 for a couple of years. Into Black is the first game that I've played where I've completely lost track of time. The movement is super smooth. The tool/weapon interface is great. The visuals are a bit anime, but with the fantastic lighting, it becomes an alien world instead of cartoonists. I've been playing for 3 days. I bought a copy for my 16 year old so we can play co-op.
Love playing this, such a cool game!

This game is fantastic. I'm totally addicted. I spend hours playing, and it flys by. I love that you can traverse great heights with the platform maker. There is also diving into deep waters filled with unique sea sharks. The bugs are tough, but fun to destroy. The mining for metals and other elements is very gratifying. I love this game!!! While hacking the cpu  stations in the game, they cleverly advertise their " Resist" game, which I will be getting now. Anything from The Binary Mill is probably going to be pretty solid. I mean I was actually sad when I completed the story. I just keep replaying. It's amazing.
you   zhong wen hao ping.
Perfect game and perfect graphics 💪🏻
This is a super fun co-op game! I hope they keep updating it and adding more.

Bought this on sale and didn’t know what to expect. It’s so much fun! I love the co op and play with my fiance. The game is longer than I expected, when I thought I would need to slow down on missions to not beat it too quickly I googled how many missions there was and I am not even half way through! The story is fun and the visuals are so cool. I will list cons below, but the game is so fun I still feel it deserves 5 stars!  

Cons: sometimes I get stuck in a wall and wouldn’t be able to get out without my fiancé’s help of creating a platform to pull myself out. The underwater movement also makes both of us sick. Movement in the game is great until under water. Also the voice actors repeat the latest dialogue every time you reload so I hear it when I load the game and when I create a co op room and after coming back from resource hunting and I get tired of hearing it lol. I wish there was a way to turn it off and click to rehear it before continuing the story.

This game is amazing. Truly immersive but not one for anyone who suffers from vertigo 😁. It has given my sweaty palms on a number of occasions.

For me, this is on par with Red Matter 2 which I didn’t think could be matched.

Give it a try, you won’t be disappointed.

reviews.review_title: It's one of my favorite vr games
reviews.review_description: The game is so much fun and looks good on quest. Note that the pcvr version does look better, but the stand alone holds it own with easier to get into. Fun with friends to help get resources for upgrades faster.

If you like space themed games, this one is for you. You gather resources to fabricate items in various series of caves, as you progress you need rarer resources to help you bring your ship back online in order to craft equipment to aid in your search and rescue mission. The storytelling starts strong but quickly brings a sense of isolation with a side helping of comedy to keep things light-hearted. Easy to get to grips with. This is not one you'll be completing within an hour. I enjoyed a minimum of 8 hours of story content. A minimum of 3 different unique biomes across at least 9 levels. 7 unique tools/equipment ranging from a Pickaxe to a Mining Laser. Lots of gadgets to help you build your own unique playstyle. There is plenty of framework to be built on for future updates or perhaps to even consider a sequel. Devs did a fabulous job with this piece of art. It's child friendly and immersive, and of course, it's not without its faults. None of them are game breaking or progress halting, and as far as bugs are concerned, there are some visual bugs when you use the mining laser on the enemies from time to time and there is another bug, not game breaking but makes play fun and keeps things interesting is, if you pass through a waterfall you can glitch the movement as if you're underwater, meaning you can adjust your altitude as if you're swimming underwater but you have the freedom to free roam the current map. This is, of course, limited to the maps, which contain waterfalls, and it's easy to fix. All you do is pass through/touch water, and you go back no "normal gravity." So again, not game breaking but fun none the less. If you're looking to scratch that resource collecting equipment upgrading space themed action platformer itch, this is the game for you. The best thing is when you finish, you can New Game+. It is fantastic!
Simply the best VR game i've ever played
Love the double jump, the story and the multiplayer aspect of the game. Lots of things to do, to collect and craft.
